notes: Spec asserts: a long-idle mistral-vibe session stays alive past readyTTL, state remains ready during the idle window, and the next user prompt resumes ready->working. vibe is a presession adapter, but — matching the codex sibling's long-idle shape — the birth is modeled UNPINNED (session_birth anchored to 'start') so a transient proc-<PID> presession OR the reconciled real session_<ts>_<hash> row (whichever the process scanner mints first) satisfies it, and the turn/idle phases anchor forward so the matcher lands the working transitions on the live session. Persistence is the daemon's adapter-agnostic PID-liveness skip: sweepStaleSnapshot (pid_manager.go:942-948) returns early when snap.pid>0 && Kill(pid,0)==nil, so the idle-past-TTL session is not deleted while the vibe process is alive; if that regresses, the idle_window invariants fire (transcript_removed for the primary session, or a flicker off ready). Recording uses IRRLICHT_READY_SESSION_TTL=15s so the 15000ms duration_at_least_ms cleanly exceeds the TTL in ~45s. BACKFLOW: the offline replay golden CANNOT reproduce vibe's meta.json sidecar (model + tokens are sidecar-only, read live), so they are UNVERIFIABLE offline — the observation vector asserts neither model nor tokens and matches kiro-cli (agent only, cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false). KNOWN_FAILING (recording 2026-07-07-22-13-22, live vibe 2.19.0): the survival core PASSES (no transcript_removed; turn 2 resumes at +22s past the 15s TTL) but idle_window FAILS its "no state_transition to working" invariant — a vibe-specific spurious force ready→working→ready flicker fires ~2s after each turn_done (evt 22:13:28.530, 22:13:50.589), with no transcript_activity of its own, after a debounce_coalesced. Sibling claudecode/codex long-idle recordings do NOT flicker, so it is the vibe multi-file (messages.jsonl + meta.json sidecar) write pattern tripping the force-first-activity classifier via the debounce flush. Daemon bug, issue prepared; when fixed, drop this flag.

text: Session appears in ready at launch (pre-session proc-<PID> or the session_<ts> id, whichever arrives first)
First user prompt drives ready -> working
Text-only assistant reply (no tool_calls) settles turn_done back to ready
Session does NOT disappear past readyTTL; the pid>0 liveness skip holds the bound session in ready during the idle
Next user prompt after the idle: ready -> working resumes normally on the same session
Resume turn completes back to ready; final session state is ready
